body.title: CLI scripts are named uumytickets/uuopentickets — not mytickets
body.result: aliases mytickets/opentickets added to ~/.bashrc; resolve to uumytickets/uuopentickets in interactive shells
body.intention: I intend that the ticket-view CLI commands work under intuitive short names.
body.description:
The scripts created in T-uu-cli-ticket-views are named `uumytickets` and `uuopentickets` (not `mytickets`/`opentickets`). User tried `mytickets` which doesn't exist.

Options:
1. Create shell aliases: `alias mytickets=uumytickets && alias opentickets=uuopentickets`
2. Rename the scripts to drop the 'uu' prefix (shorter)
3. Document the correct names in a README or shell init snippet

User requested the 'uu' prefix ('they're on the path') so option 1 (aliases in ~/.bashrc or ~/.zshrc) is probably the right call.

**Affected files:** ~/bin/uumytickets, ~/bin/uuopentickets, ~/.bashrc or ~/.zshrc (for aliases)
**Design rules:** CLI usability — names should be memorable and match what the user expects
**Scope boundary:** Add aliases so both `mytickets` and `uumytickets` work; verify from a fresh shell
**Completion criteria:** Both `mytickets` and `opentickets` commands work; `which mytickets` points to the script; aliased from ~/.bashrc/.zshrc
